Reason_for_Visit.summary: 59 y.o. female with ER-Positive PR-Positive HER2-Negative right breast cancer, status post right lumpectomy of two sites and sentinel lymph node biopsy, for medical oncology consultation

Clinical_Findings.findings: Pathology: Tumor size: 1.6 cm and 0.6 cm, margins negative for invasive cancer but positive for DCIS, lymph node status: micrometastatic carcinoma in one lymph node (0.5 mm). Imaging: MRI shows suspicious mass in the upper outer quadrant of the right breast with satellite masses and surrounding non-mass enhancement. Physical exam: Periareolar incision clean and dry, erythematous dermatitis reaction in right axilla, small seroma in lower inner quadrant, no palpable masses or tenderness in left breast.

Treatment_Changes.supportive_meds: Acetaminophen 1000mg PO every 8 hours, Ibuprofen 600mg PO 3 times daily

Treatment_Goals.response_assessment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.
Treatment_Goals.current_meds: letrozole 2.5mg PO daily, acetaminophen 1000mg every 8 hours, ibuprofen 600mg 3 times daily

Response_Assessment.response_assessment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.
Response_Assessment.response_to_treatment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.

hormonal therapy.Long term side_effects_discussed: possible acceleration of bone density loss

bone therapy.summary: Bone density scan can be completed when she returns from travel, may start bisphosphonate if osteopenia

radiotherapy.summary: In regards to her radiation therapy plan, she is at risk for local recurrence in her right breast for which ***** is recommending additional radiation.

other treatment.summary: Calcium supplementation with 1200mg/day combination of diet and supplement, vitamin D supplement pending

Medication_Plan.medication_plan: Start letrozole 2.5mg PO daily, calcium supplementation with 1200mg/day combination of diet and supplement, vitamin D supplement pending, consider bisphosphonate pending bone scan result

Therapy_plan.therapy_plan: Start letrozole 2.5mg PO daily. Continue use of vaginal moisturizer. Plan to start bisphosphonate if bone density scan shows osteopenia. Plan to discuss vaginal estrogens at future visit. Plan to pursue radiation therapy in the future, specifically for local recurrence in the right breast, but not for regional lymph nodes.
Therapy_plan.bisphosphonate_start: if bone density scan shows osteopenia
Therapy_plan.radiation_therapy: in the future, specifically for local recurrence in the right breast, but not for regional lymph nodes

radiotherapy_plan.radiotherapy_plan: Additional radiation to the right breast is recommended due to risk of local recurrence, but not to regional lymph nodes.
